cancellation_policies.name: Standard 24-hour Policy
cancellation_policies.description: Cancellations or reschedules made less than 24 hours before the appointment start time are charged 50% of the session fee. Appointments canceled with 24 hours or more notice are not charged.

cancellation_policies.name: Same-Day & No-Show Policy
cancellation_policies.description: Same-day cancellations (within 4 hours of the appointment) and no-shows are charged the full session fee.

cancellation_policies.name: Group Session 48-hour Policy
cancellation_policies.description: For group therapy and support groups, cancellations within 48 hours of the group start time are charged a $25 late cancellation fee.

resources.title: 5 Grounding Techniques for Panic Attacks
resources.summary: Learn five simple grounding skills you can use anywhere to calm your body and mind during a panic attack.
resources.content:
Panic attacks can feel overwhelming, but there are concrete skills that can help you ride the wave. In this article, we’ll cover five grounding techniques you can practice now and use when panic shows up.

1. **5-4-3-2-1 Senses Exercise**: Name 5 things you can see, 4 things you can feel, 3 things you can hear, 2 things you can smell, and 1 thing you can taste. Move slowly through each step, really noticing each sensation.

2. **Feet-on-the-floor**: Sit with both feet on the ground. Press your feet gently into the floor, notice the pressure, and feel the support beneath you. Pair this with slow exhales.

3. **Temperature change**: Splash cool water on your face or hold an ice cube in your hand. Notice the changing sensations as it melts.

Practice these techniques regularly when you’re calm so they’re easier to access when panic hits.

resources.title: Calm Breathing Plan for Panic
resources.summary: A printable worksheet to help you build a personalized slow-breathing routine for panic and anxiety spikes.
resources.content:
Use this worksheet to design a realistic, personalized breathing plan you can rely on during panic:

- **Step 1: Choose your count** (for example, inhale for 4, exhale for 6). Longer exhales help signal safety to the nervous system.
- **Step 2: Identify situations** where panic often shows up (e.g., on public transit, before meetings, at bedtime).
- **Step 3: Create a practice schedule** to rehearse your breathing plan once or twice a day when you’re not panicking.
- **Step 4: Write a coping statement** (e.g., "This will pass; I know what to do.") to pair with your breathing.

Print this worksheet and keep a copy on your phone or in your bag.

resources.title: Understanding the Panic Attack Cycle
resources.summary: A short video explaining how the panic cycle works and why it can feel like it comes out of nowhere.
resources.content: In this 9-minute video, a licensed therapist walks through the common panic attack cycle: trigger, body sensations, catastrophic thoughts, and the relief/avoidance loop. You’ll learn why panic can seem sudden, how fear of fear keeps the cycle going, and what it means to gently lean into, rather than fight, sensations.

resources.title: Managing Anxiety at Work: A Practical Guide
resources.summary: Concrete strategies to manage anxiety and overwhelm in fast-paced work environments.
resources.content:
Work can be a major source of anxiety, especially when expectations are high and downtime is scarce. This guide offers practical strategies you can start using this week:

- **Name your stressors**: Separate what you can control (your schedule, boundaries, self-talk) from what you can’t (other people’s reactions, market changes).
- **Use micro-breaks**: 60–120 second breaks to stretch, breathe, or look away from the screen help reset your nervous system.
- **Set realistic goals**: Break tasks into 15–30 minute chunks, and define “good enough” instead of chasing perfection.
- **Communicate needs**: Scripts and examples are included for talking with managers or HR about workload and accommodations.

Experiment with one small change at a time and notice what actually shifts your anxiety level.

resources.title: Wind-Down Routine for Better Sleep
resources.summary: A 15-minute guided audio to help you transition from work mode to rest and reduce insomnia.
resources.content: This 15-minute audio guides you through gentle stretches, a short body scan, and breathing to help your nervous system shift gears from work to rest. Use it as part of a consistent wind-down routine, ideally at the same time each night.

resources.title: First Steps in Healing from Trauma: Stabilization Skills
resources.summary: An overview of early trauma recovery skills, including grounding, safety planning, and building support.
resources.content:
Trauma healing often begins with stabilization: building enough safety and support to manage daily life. This article covers foundational skills such as grounding, orienting to the present moment, creating a simple safety plan, and beginning to identify supportive people in your world.

You’ll also learn why it’s often helpful to focus on stabilization before diving into detailed trauma memories.

resources.title: Is a Trauma Support Group Right for Me?
resources.summary: Answers to common questions about joining a trauma-focused therapy or support group.
resources.content:
Group therapy can feel intimidating, especially when it focuses on trauma. This FAQ covers what to expect, how much you’re invited to share, confidentiality, and how facilitators keep the group emotionally safe.

We also outline the difference between psychoeducational trauma groups and processing groups, and what a typical session looks like in our practice.

resources.title: What to Expect in Your First Therapy Session
resources.summary: A brief guide to reduce uncertainty and anxiety before starting therapy.
resources.content:
Starting therapy can bring up a mix of hope and nervousness. This article walks you through what typically happens in a first session: reviewing consent and privacy, talking about your goals, asking questions about your history, and collaborating on a plan.

We also offer tips on how to know if a therapist is a good fit, and what to do if it doesn’t feel right.

resources.title: Work Boundary Setting Worksheet
resources.summary: A step-by-step worksheet to help you identify and communicate healthier work boundaries.
resources.content: Use this worksheet to map out your current work boundaries, identify what feels unsustainable, and script specific phrases you can use with managers, coworkers, or clients.

resources.title: Sleep Hygiene Checklist
resources.summary: A simple checklist to help you track and improve habits that affect your sleep quality.
resources.content: This checklist covers daytime and evening habits that influence sleep, including caffeine intake, screen time, exercise, and bedroom environment.

resources.title: External Resource: National Panic Disorder Information Center
resources.summary: A curated external link to a reputable organization offering additional education on panic disorder.
resources.content: This link directs you to a trusted external organization with in-depth information, personal stories, and treatment options for panic disorder.

resources.title: Leaders’ Guide to Supporting Employee Mental Health
resources.summary: Key principles for managers who want to foster a mentally healthy workplace.
resources.content: Designed for supervisors and HR professionals, this guide explains how to notice warning signs of burnout, respond to disclosures, and create psychological safety on teams.

resources.title: 3-Minute Mindfulness Reset
resources.summary: A brief guided mindfulness practice you can use between meetings or before bed.
resources.content: This short audio invites you to notice your breathing, observe thoughts without judgment, and reconnect with the present moment.

resources.title: Finding Therapy in Your Preferred Language
resources.summary: Tips for locating therapists who speak your language, including Spanish and other commonly requested languages.
resources.content: Language can be a key part of feeling understood in therapy. This article highlights directories and questions to ask when seeking care in your preferred language.

resources.title: Draft: Coping with Seasonal Mood Changes
resources.summary: Draft article on understanding and coping with seasonal affective shifts.
resources.content: This draft is under review and not yet available publicly.

service_fees.label: Individual Therapy (50 minutes)
service_fees.description: Standard 50-minute individual psychotherapy sessions. Limited sliding-scale slots are available based on income and financial need.

service_fees.label: Individual Telehealth – Anxiety & Panic (50 minutes)
service_fees.description: Specialized individual telehealth sessions focused on anxiety, panic attacks, and related concerns.

service_fees.label: Couples Counseling (50 minutes)
service_fees.description: 50-minute couples counseling sessions focused on communication, conflict, and life transitions. Fees are per couple, not per person.

service_fees.label: Trauma Support Group (90 minutes)
service_fees.description: Weekly 90-minute trauma support groups with 6–8 participants. Sliding-scale options keep the per-session fee at $50 or below.

service_fees.label: Workplace Mental Health Workshop (Half-day Virtual)
service_fees.description: Half-day virtual workplace mental health workshop for teams, including education, discussion, and practical tools. Pricing may vary for larger groups or custom content.

workshops.name: Workplace Mental Health Essentials
workshops.description: A practical workshop for teams covering stress, burnout, and how to create a more mentally healthy workplace. Includes interactive exercises and time for Q&A.

workshops.name: Supporting Your Team: Mental Health for Managers
workshops.description: Designed for people leaders, this workshop focuses on recognizing signs of distress, having supportive conversations, and navigating accommodations.

workshops.name: School Mental Health 101
workshops.description: A training for school staff on recognizing and responding to student mental health concerns, with age-appropriate strategies.

workshops.name: Community Talk: Stress & Resilience
workshops.description: A community-focused presentation on understanding stress, building resilience, and accessing local mental health resources.

therapists.bio: Dr. Morales is a clinical psychologist who specializes in anxiety, panic attacks, and work-related stress for adults. She uses CBT and mindfulness-based approaches and offers telehealth-only care to increase accessibility for busy professionals.

Carolina works with teens, young adults, and families navigating anxiety, cultural stress, and communication challenges. As a bilingual Spanish/English therapist, she offers a warm, direct style and maintains several sliding-scale spots for teen clients.

James supports adults dealing with chronic stress, burnout, and anxiety related to demanding careers. He integrates CBT, ACT, and practical skills to help clients create sustainable work-life boundaries.
Dr. Desai specializes in trauma, PTSD, and dissociation. She offers both individual therapy and facilitates trauma-focused groups, drawing from EMDR, parts work, and somatic approaches.
Miguel works with adults and couples around anxiety, communication, and major life changes. He offers sessions in both English and Spanish and brings a collaborative, strengths-based approach.

Sara focuses on couples counseling and relationship concerns, including communication, conflict, and life transitions such as becoming parents. She also works with individuals on attachment and relational patterns.
Emily specializes in teen and young adult mental health, focusing on anxiety, depression, and school or college stress. She offers a collaborative style that blends CBT and creative approaches.
Dr. Cohen focuses on insomnia, sleep disorders, and anxiety. She uses CBT-I and evidence-based strategies to help clients improve sleep and reduce nighttime worry.

Luis is a bilingual therapist who works primarily with Latino teens and young adults around identity, anxiety, and family relationships. He offers sliding-scale options for youth and college students.

Aisha facilitates trauma support and anxiety skills groups and also works individually with adults healing from trauma and chronic stress. Her approach emphasizes safety, pacing, and practical coping tools.

therapy_groups.name: Online Trauma Skills & Support Group (Evenings)
therapy_groups.description: A 12-week, skills-focused trauma support group for adults who have experienced single-incident or chronic trauma. Sessions emphasize stabilization, grounding, and building coping tools rather than detailed trauma narratives.

therapy_groups.name: In-Person Trauma Stabilization Group
therapy_groups.description: An in-person group for adults focusing on early-stage trauma recovery: grounding, safety planning, and building present-day resources.
therapy_groups.location: SoMa Office, 5th St., San Francisco, CA 94107

therapy_groups.name: Teen Trauma & Resilience Group
therapy_groups.description: A closed 8-week group for teens (ages 14–17) who have experienced trauma and are seeking peer support and coping skills. Includes parent check-ins at the beginning and end of the cycle.
therapy_groups.location: Mission District Office, 24th St., San Francisco, CA 94110

therapy_groups.name: Ongoing Trauma Process Group (Adults)
therapy_groups.description: A long-term process group for adults with prior individual trauma therapy experience who are ready for deeper relational work and sharing. Intake interview required.
therapy_groups.location: SoMa Office, 5th St., San Francisco, CA 94107

therapy_groups.name: Completed Trauma Skills Group (Spring 2025)
therapy_groups.description: Archived listing for record-keeping only. A past 10-week trauma skills group that ran in Spring 2025.

therapy_groups.name: Evening Anxiety Support Group for Professionals
therapy_groups.description: A supportive group for working adults experiencing generalized anxiety and work-related stress. Focus on practical CBT and mindfulness tools, as well as peer connection.

therapy_groups.name: Panic Attack Skills & Exposure Group (Online)
therapy_groups.description: A structured 8-week CBT group focusing on panic attacks, avoidance patterns, and gradual exposure. Includes weekly between-session practice.

therapy_groups.name: Work Stress & Burnout Recovery Group
therapy_groups.description: A group for adults experiencing chronic work stress, burnout, or compassion fatigue. Focus on boundaries, values-based decision making, and nervous system regulation.

therapy_groups.name: CBT-I Group for Insomnia
therapy_groups.description: A structured, time-limited group based on Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-I) to help adults improve sleep and reduce nighttime anxiety.

therapy_groups.name: LGBTQIA+ Adult Support Group
therapy_groups.description: An affirming space for LGBTQIA+ adults to explore identity, relationships, minority stress, and resilience.
therapy_groups.location: Mission District Office, Valencia St., San Francisco, CA 94110

therapy_groups.name: Mindful Teen Anxiety Group (Online)
therapy_groups.description: A skills-based group for teens (ages 13–17) to learn mindfulness, emotion regulation, and peer support for anxiety and school stress.

therapy_groups.name: Trauma Support Group (Waitlist Only)
therapy_groups.description: A recurring trauma support group that is currently full. Prospective members may join the waitlist to be notified when space opens or a new cycle begins.

appointment_requests.client_message: Im looking for online therapy to help with panic attacks that have been happening at work and on my commute.
Im a working professional dealing with significant work stress and anxiety. I prefer evening telehealth sessions after 6 PM.
Were seeking couples counseling to work on communication and conflict, ideally via evening telehealth sessions.
We need to cancel our upcoming couples session on March 14 at 3:00 PM. We understand that the late cancellation fee in your policy will apply.
Im looking for in-person counseling for my teen around anxiety and school stress. Spanish-language sessions would be helpful.
Im interested in joining the upcoming online trauma skills & support group that meets Thursdays at 6 PM. Please let me know if there is space or if I should be added to the waitlist.
